DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF BUSH STREET PIZZA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS MOOT AS AN ADDENDUM HAS BEEN PRODUCED. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THAT ADDENDUM ON THIS MOTION. HOWEVER, COUNSEL ARE ADVISED THAT THE COURT WOULD NOT BE LIKELY TO FIND THE SECOND ADDENDUM TO BE SUFFICIENT. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INITIAL RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $300, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF BUSH STREET PIZZA, INCORPORATED D B A ESCAPE FROM NEW YORK'S Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEK LEAVE TO FILE CROSS COMPLAINT. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/AY

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Bush Street Pizza, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the initial responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $400, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Request For Productin Of Documents, Set One.
Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Bush Street Pizza, INc.'s Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, is moot as an addendum has been produced. The court will not determine the adequacy of that addendum on this motion. However, counsel are advised that the court would not be likely to find the second addendum to be sufficient. The court finds that the initial responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $300, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
